PENDLETON. Ore, May 1O.-In-
formation from Congressman Jones
of Washington says that the secre-
tary of the interior will allow whites
to lease Yakima Indian reservation
lands on the following terms


THAT all leases contain a provision
for levying land for cultivation on
about 40 of each S0-acre tract: for an
alfalfa crop in rotation with a sugar
acres in each allotment to be culti-
waited by the allottees; for the en-
ployment of Indians in the beet fields
and in the factory wherever practical
BILE; for the erection on each allotment
of certain permanent improvements
to the value of $500. the improve
ments to be located at such point
on the reserved portion as will be
contiguous and most convenient to
the remainder of the allotment, the
same to be left in good condition at
the end of the lease and to become
the property of the allottee.


mo other permanent improvements
whatsoever that is in the nature OF
factories or other buildings for con-
Quoting the sugar beet industry are
to be permitted on the allotments. An
annual rental of 81 per acre is asked,
with further provision that the rental
may be readjusted In five years IL
the secretary sees fit to do so, all
though the leases may run 10 years."
